quotes.text: The gathering of the Jews to Palestine is one of the most outstanding and significant of all the signs of the times.
quotes.speaker: Mark E. Petersen
quotes.attr: Mark E. Petersen, in Conference Report, Oct. 1965, 61; or Improvement Era, Dec. 1965, 1128

quotes.text: I will bring again the captivity" means, "I will return the captives.
quotes.speaker: Daniel H. Ludlow
quotes.source: A Companion to Your Study of the Old Testament
quotes.attr: Daniel H. Ludlow, A Companion to Your Study of the Old Testament, 324

notes: These verses (through the middle of verse 21) were quoted by Olivery Cowdery as being among those which the angel Moroni told Joseph Smith were about to be fulfilled (see Messenger and Advocate, Apr. 1835, pp. 110-111).

The first part of chapter 31 contains a prophecy about the gathering of Israel. Parts of this prophecy are directed specifically to Ephraim, one of the ten tribes that were lost and scattered after the destruction of the kingdom of Israel by the Assyrians in 721 B.C. Clearly this vision is intended for the latter days. Once again, the key words from Jeremiah's call occur (Jer. 1:10): "build," in regard to the "virgin of Israel," an appellation often used for a city, and "plant," referring to the vines of Samaria (Jer. 31:4-5). Oliver Cowdery, in his summary of the things the angel Moroni taught Joseph Smith, quoted Jeremiah 31:6: "For there shall be a day, that the watchmen upon the mount Ephraim shall cry, Arise ye, and let us go up to Zion unto the Lord our God." The central event of this prophecy is the gathering: "Behold, I will bring them from the north country, and gather them from the coasts of the earth" (Jer. 31:8); and "Hear the word of the Lord, O ye nations, and declare it in the isles afar off, and say, He that scattered Israel will gather him, and keep him, as a shepherd doth his flock" (Jer. 31:10). The second half of Jeremiah 31 contains a prophecy directed to Judah and Israel - one of the most significant of all of the prophecies in Jeremiah (see a similar prophecy in Ezek. 11:19; 36:26-27). Several passages from this prophecy, according to Oliver Cowdery, were quoted or paraphrased to Joseph Smith by Moroni. In Jeremiah 31:28, the Lord recounted the call of Jeremiah and noted that as the destruction

quotes.text: A more literal translation from the Hebrew for this verse would be: "The fierce anger of Jehovah shall not turn back until he has finished and until he has fulfilled the intentions of his heart. In the latter days you will understand it.
quotes.speaker: Daniel H. Ludlow
quotes.source: A Companion to Your Study of the Old Testament
quotes.attr: Daniel H. Ludlow, A Companion to Your Study of the Old Testament, 324
